name: Paul Bascomb
abstract: Paul Bascomb (February 12, 1912 – December 2, 1986) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist, noted for his extended tenure with big band leader Erskine Hawkins. He is a 1979 inductee of the Alabama Jazz Hall of Fame.

concerts.label: American Opera Company of New York
Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Charity Fund's 7th Annual Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Paul Bascomb was the tenor player's name, when he first came out of prison... This band was From Alabama State, Erskine Hawkins' band... I heard all these people, you know. I used to listen to them on the records

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: One was called El Sino, where a tenor player named Paul Bascomb, who used to be with Erskine Hawkins, a famous band, played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: The only name band I'd known him to be in was Erskine Hawkins. ... Yeah, I remember that.

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: His name was Paul [Bascomb]. He was famous for – he was executing, at that time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah. Well, then Bascomb didn't stay in there very long, then. He was just --
